rationale_for_baseline_tier: A previously fit young adult with new post-viral chest pain, resting tachycardia disproportionate to a low-grade fever, progressive dyspnoea and early heart-failure signs (S3 gallop, raised JVP, peripheral oedema) has suspected acute myocarditis. This can progress unpredictably and rapidly to fulminant heart failure, cardiogenic shock or a lethal ventricular arrhythmia, and the patient lives alone with no one to summon help. Self-exertion (even walking) is dangerous, so self-transport is unsafe. An emergency ambulance (000) to the Emergency Department for continuous monitoring is the only appropriate tier — mirroring the source note's arranged ambulance transfer.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening or sudden severe breathlessness before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.rationale: Rapidly worsening breathlessness signals acute decompensation of the failing heart and mandates upgrading ambulance priority.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing suddenly gets much worse, call 000 straight back and tell them your breathing is deteriorating so they upgrade the ambulance.

escalation_edges.rationale: Collapse may signal a life-threatening arrhythmia or cardiogenic shock; paramedic resuscitation is the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel you're about to pass out, call 000 immediately and stay on the line; unlock your door now so paramedics can reach you if you collapse.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rapid fluttering or pounding in the chest (palpitations suggesting arrhythmia)
escalation_edges.rationale: New palpitations in myocarditis may herald a dangerous ventricular arrhythmia requiring emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your heart suddenly starts fluttering or pounding rapidly, call 000 right away and tell them your heart rhythm has changed.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe or crushing chest pain, or chest pain spreading to the arm or jaw with sweating
escalation_edges.rationale: Escalating chest pain could reflect worsening myocardial injury or an alternative acute coronary event and warrants immediate emergency response.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the chest pain becomes severe or spreads to your arm or jaw with sweating, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with no one present to witness a sudden deterioration
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises urgency and makes self-transport unsafe: an ambulance is required both for monitoring en route and because an unwitnessed arrhythmic collapse at home could be fatal. Advise unlocking the front door for paramedics and, if possible, alerting a neighbour or contact to check in.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance now and tell them you have chest pain, a racing heart and breathlessness after a recent viral illness so they treat it as a possible heart emergency. Stay resting — do not walk or exert yourself at all, and let the paramedics move you. Unlock your front door so they can get in.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or the next in-hours period.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary with the ECG, troponin, echo/MRI findings and exercise-restriction advice — take these to your GP and cardiology follow-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treats the picture as benign post-viral fatigue, anxiety or musculoskeletal chest pain and advises home rest or routine/prompt GP review rather than emergency assessment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Undetected fulminant myocarditis progresses to a sudden ventricular arrhythmia or cardiogenic shock; the patient lives alone and collapses unwitnessed, risking death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognises the seriousness and directs to ED but advises self-transport rather than an ambulance, so exertion or an unmonitored journey could precipitate an arrhythmia with no paramedic support available.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact cardiac diagnosis (myocarditis vs pericarditis vs ACS vs PE) cannot be established over telehealth without an ECG, troponin and imaging.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the worst credible possibility — escalate for urgent monitored emergency assessment — rather than waiting for diagnostic certainty.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using diagnostic uncertainty, or the patient's youth and recent virus, to justify reassurance and home management.
